questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\frac{{ }^n C_1+3^{2 n} C_3+5^{2 n} C_5+\ldots}{n(n+1)}=2^{n-\alpha} \forall n \geq 3, n \in N$ then $\alpha$ is equal to $\qquad$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & n(x-1)^{n-1}+n(n-1) x(x-1)^{n-2}=n^n C_n x^{n-1}-(n-1)^{2 n} C_{n-1} x^{n-2}+\ldots+(-1)^{n-1}{ }^n C_1...(1) \\ & \text { also } n(x+1)^{n-1}+n(n-1) x(x+1)^{n-2}={ }^n C_1+2^{2 n} C_2 x+\ldots+n^2 C_{11} x^{n-1}....(2) \\ & \text { Puting } x=1 \text { and adding (1) and (2) we get } 1^{2 n} C_1+3^{2 n} C_3+5^2{ }^n C_5+\ldots=n(n+1) 2^{n-3} \\ & \text { Hence } \alpha=3 \text {. }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{S}={ }^{100} \mathrm{C}_0{ }^{100} \mathrm{C}_2+{ }^{100} \mathrm{C}_4{ }^{100} \mathrm{C}_6+\ldots+{ }^{100} \mathrm{C}_{96}{ }^{100} \mathrm{C}_{98} \\ & =\text { Coefficient of } \mathrm{x}^0 \text { in }(1+\mathrm{x})^{100} \frac{(1+\mathrm{x})^{100}}{\mathrm{x}^2}+\frac{(1+\mathrm{x})^{100}}{\mathrm{x}^4} \frac{(1+\mathrm{x})^{100}}{\mathrm{x}^6}+\ldots+\frac{(1+\mathrm{x})^{100}}{\mathrm{x}^{90}} \frac{(1+\mathrm{x})^{100}}{\mathrm{x}^{98}} \\ & =\text { Coefficient of } \mathrm{x}^0 \text { in } \frac{(1+\mathrm{x})^{200}\left(1-\mathrm{x}^{200}\right)}{\mathrm{x}^{194}\left(1-\mathrm{x}^8\right)} \\ & =\text { Coefficient of } \mathrm{x}^{194} \text { in }(1+\mathrm{x})^{200}\left(1-\mathrm{x}^8\right)^{-1}={ }^{200} \mathrm{C}_2+{ }^{200} \mathrm{C}_{10}+\ldots+{ }^{200} \mathrm{C}_{194} .\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider two polynomial $f(x)$ and $g(x)$ given by $f(x)=\sum_{r=0}^{200} \beta_r x^r$ and $g(x)=\sum_{r=0}^{200} \alpha_r x^r$ such that $\beta_r=1$ $\forall 100 \leq \mathrm{r} \leq 200$ and $f(x+1)=g(x)$. Let $A=\sum_{r=100}^{200} \alpha_r$, then the remainder when $A$ is divided by 15 is equal to $\qquad$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \sum_{r=0}^{200} \alpha_r x^r=\sum_{r=0}^{200} \beta_r(1+x)^r \\ & \alpha_0+\alpha_1 x+\ldots .+\alpha_{200} x^{200}=\beta_0+\beta_1(1+x)+\ldots .+\beta_{200}(1+x)^{200} \\ & =\beta_0+\beta_1(1+x)+\ldots .+\beta_{99}(1+x)^{99}+\frac{(1+x)^{201}-(1+x)^{100}}{x}\end{aligned}$
Equating coefficient of $x^{100}, x^{101}, \ldots ., x^{200}$ respectively we get
$\begin{aligned} & \alpha_{100}={ }^{201} C_{101}, \alpha_{101}={ }^{201} C_{102}, \ldots ., \alpha_{200}={ }^{201} C_{201} \\ & \Rightarrow A=\sum_{r=100}^{200} \alpha_r=\alpha_{100}+\alpha_{101}+\ldots . .+\alpha_{200}=2^{200}\end{aligned}$
When $2^{200}$ is divided by 15 we get remainder 1
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: If the coefficient of $x^8$ in the expansion of $\left(2+9 x^3+6 x^4+x^5\right)^{10}$ is $5 \cdot 2^p \cdot 3^q$ where $p, q \in N$, then find the value of $(p+q)$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \left(2+x^3(3+x)^2\right)^{10} \\ & \downarrow \\ & { }^{10} C_r \cdot 2^{10-r} \cdot x^{3 r}(3+x)^{2 r} \\ & \downarrow \\ & \quad{ }^{2 r} C_k \cdot 3^{2 r-k} \cdot x^k \\ & 3 r+k=8 \Rightarrow k=2, r=2 \\ & \therefore \text { Coefficient of } x^8={ }^{10} C_2 \cdot 2^8 \cdot{ }^4 C_2 \cdot 3^2=5 \cdot 2^9 \cdot 3^5\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
If $\left(x-2+\frac{1}{x}\right)^{30}=n_0 x^{30}+n_1 x^{29}+\ldots \ldots+n_{29} x+n_{30}+n_{31} x^{-1}+\ldots \ldots+n_{60} x^{-30}$ and $C=n_0+n_1+n_2+\ldots \ldots+n_{60}$. Find the value of $(a+b)$ if $C-n_{30}=-\left(\begin{array}{l}a \\ b\end{array}\right)$.
[Note: $\left(\begin{array}{l}n \\ r\end{array}\right)$ denotes ${ }^n C_r$. ]
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \left(x-2+\frac{1}{x}\right)^{30}=n_0 x^{30}+n_1 x^{29}+\ldots \ldots .+n_{29} x+n_{30}+n_{31} x^{-1}+\ldots \ldots .+n_{60} x^{-30} \\
& (x-1)^{60}=n_0 x^{60}+n_1 x^{59}+\ldots \ldots .+n_{29} x^{31}+n_{30} x^{30}+n_{31} x^{29}+\ldots \ldots .+n_{60} \\
& \therefore n_0={ }^{60} C_0, n_1=-{ }^{60} C_1, n_2={ }^{60} C_2, \ldots \ldots . n_{30}={ }^{60} C_{30} \ldots . . n_{60}={ }^{60} C_{60} \\
& \therefore C=n_0+n_1+n_2+\ldots \ldots .+n_{60}=0
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence, $C-n_{30}=-\left(\begin{array}{l}a \\ b\end{array}\right)$
$$
\begin{array}{ll}
\Rightarrow & 0-1 \cdot\left({ }^{60} C_{30}\right) \\
\therefore & a=60, b=30
\end{array}
$$

Hence, $a+b=90$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: If term independent of $x$ in the expansion of $\left(x^2-\frac{3}{x}\right)^6$ is $\lambda 3^5$, then find the value of $\lambda$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} T_{r+1} & ={ }^6 C_r \cdot\left(x^2\right)^{6-r} \cdot\left(\frac{-3}{x}\right)^r \\ 12-3 r & =0 \\ r \quad r & =4 \\ T_5 & ={ }^6 C_4 \cdot(-3)^4 \Rightarrow 5 \cdot 3^5\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $a$ and $b$ be the coefficient of $x^3$ in $\left(1+x+2 x^2+3 x^3\right)^4$ and $\left(1+x+2 x^2+3 x^3+4 x^4\right)^4$ respectively. Find the value of $(a-b)$.
questions.solution:
We have $b=$ coefficient of $x^3$ in $\left(\left(1+x+2 x^2+3 x^3\right)+4 x^4\right)^4$
$\begin{aligned}
& =\text { coefficient of } x^3 \text { in }\left[{ }^4 C_0\left(1+x+2 x^2+3 x^3\right)^4\left(4 x^4\right)^0+{ }^4 C_1\left(1+x+2 x^2+3 x^3\right)^3\left(4 x^4\right)^1+\ldots \ldots . . . .\right] \\
& =\text { coefficient of } x^3 \text { in }\left(1+x+2 x^2+3 x^3\right)^4=a
\end{aligned}$
Hence $\mathrm{a}-\mathrm{b}=0$ Ans.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
